items.title: Farrer presents One Nation as a genuine electoral threat rather than just a protest
items.summary: The result in Farrer has made One Nation's attempts to become the natural home for conservative Australians look less like a protest and more like a genuine electoral threat.

items.title: From stealing cars to acting: How Tropfest founder John Polson channelled his chaotic past
items.summary: John Polson was a troubled teen when a sliding doors moment led him into the world of acting. He went on to create Tropfest, one of the world's biggest short film festivals, changing his life and the Australian industry.

items.title: Confounding bankers' assumptions, the RBA's post-COVID rate hikes caused Australians to work more, not less
items.summary: The International Monetary Fund found that thousands of Australians took on a second or third job when the RBA hiked rates rapidly in 2022 and 2023. It expected the opposite.

items.title: Councils to get federal funds for roads, pipes and wires in housing push
items.summary: Treasurer Jim Chalmers will reveal details of the $2 billion fund in Tuesday's federal budget, declaring the cash injection will add an extra 65,000 homes over a decade.

items.title: Iran is trying to formalise its control over the Strait of Hormuz, but there are some obvious problems
items.summary: Amid a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, the IRGC has launched a maritime authority for the Strait of Hormuz. The Persian Gulf Strait Authority says ships need to email their application for passage. So, we sent them a request.

items.title: Mother at the NGV explores artists' experiences of motherhood throughout history
items.summary: Mother at Melbourne's NGV offers contrasting depictions of motherhood, from Toscani's Madonna and Child to First Nations weaving.

items.title: Cheat Sheet: How Big Brother embedded into Australian culture
items.summary: Imported to Australia 25 years ago, Big Brother introduced the country to the concept of 24-hour monitoring as entertainment.

items.title: Quiz: Think you've got a way with words? Guy Montgomery's here with your Sunday spelling quiz
items.summary: Ready to hone your homophones and verbalise your vowels? Let's get spelling!

items.title: One Nation scores historic win in Farrer by-election
items.summary: One Nation scores an historic win in the Farrer by-election, marking the first time the party has won a Lower House seat in federal parliament.

items.title: Putin attends scaled-back World War II victory parade as worries deepen in Russia over war in Ukraine
items.summary: Once used to show off Russia's vast military, including its nuclear-capable missiles, this year's Victory Day ​parade had no tanks or other military equipment ‌rolling over the cobblestones of Red Square.

items.title: Melbourne City books A-League Women's grand final spot after stunning Shelby McMahon goal
items.summary: Shelby McMahon fires Melbourne City into the A-League Women's grand final, with her side downing Melbourne Victory 2-0 on aggregate.

items.title: Enormous ruby discovered in conflict-torn Myanmar
items.summary: Miners have unearthed a large ruby in Myanmar, which produces up to 90 per cent of the world's rubies.

items.title: Record beef exports to the United States as prices soar ahead of 'grilling season'
items.summary: The United States is importing more beef from Australia as its own supplies dwindle and demand soars.

items.title: Police hold concerns for welfare of Victorian man Samuel Whitsed, 27, last seen near Western Australia's Bibbulmun track
items.summary: Samuel Whitsed, 27, was last seen on February 18 after he was dropped off near the popular hiking trail in Perth's south-eastern fringe, and police continue searching for him.

items.title: Tensions high ahead of Putin's parade as Australia told to evacuate Kyiv embassy
items.summary: The Australian embassy in Ukraine is on high alert after the Kremlin warned staff to evacuate ahead of possible "massive missile strikes" this weekend, as Moscow prepares to hold a scaled-down military parade.

items.title: Origin of Australian term 'willy-willy' links back to Pilbara First Nations
items.summary: It's not unusual to see willy-willies kicking up dust across WA, but did you know the name of the weather phenomenon comes from WA's north-west?

items.title: If you are not convinced by sardines, this is the dish to try - ABC News
items.summary: This simple and nutritious pasta dish is sure to satisfy those who already adore sardines, and it may just convert those who don't.

items.title: Cheesemaker Philippa Abela embodies a lifetime of Maltese cheese-crafting
items.summary: Eighty-year-old cheesemaker Philippa Abela has been practising a craft passed down by her mother, who emigrated with her from Malta when Philippa was a child.

items.title: Victor Harbor tramway Clydesdales battle flu-like illness stopping tourist service
items.summary: The majestic horses that pull the historic tramway at Victor Harbor on the South Australian coastline are under the weather. Locals hope they will soon return.

items.title: New bug species Larrakia feather-legged assassin discovered in national park near Darwin

items.title: UFO files released by US President Donald Trump as part of 'unprecedented transparency'

items.title: Tasmania rejected an on‑country hearing but other courts show it works

items.title: If a week is a long time in politics, Julie Bishop's resignation as ANU chancellor shows six months is a lifetime at a university
items.summary: December 31 was the date when Julie Bishop was due to finish her term as Australian National University chancellor, so why — after having repeatedly stated she would see her commitment through — did she leave months early?

items.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2 hopes you're as distracted as you are nostalgic
items.summary: Miranda Priestly has lost her fangs in the reboot as weak plot lines make room for superfluous scenes and characters.

items.title: Australia is breaking its carbon bond, but there's a cost
items.summary: As Australia weans itself away from coal, aging power stations across the country have been reduced to smoke and rubble. But for communities at the coalface, there are serious and dramatic consequences.

items.title: Alex De Minaur destroys racquet in Italian Open exit as Novak Djokovic also out

items.title: Australia's only daily Auslan news service by Deaf journalists to end
items.summary: News delivered by Deaf journalists covering international wars, elections, cyclones, and fuel shortages is set to wind up next month due to a federal government grant ending.

items.title: Mother's Day complicated and full of love for kids in foster care

items.title: Voters in Farrer welcome by-election day after protracted campaign

items.title: Tough talk on ISIS-linked families won't fix Australia's terrorism problem

items.title: Some people will hate Tuesday's federal budget but Jim Chalmers doesn't mind
items.summary: For once, the budget won't be a dull one. Jim Chalmers's tax reforms will create a stir and some question whether they will close the generation gap, but the treasurer is projecting a relaxed figure.

items.title: Today in History, May 9: The 'magnificent' opening of Australia's first federal parliament
items.summary: More than 12,000 people packed into Melbourne's Royal Exhibition Building to witness the "pomp and ceremony" of a "magnificent" occasion in the country's history.

items.title: Hostages freed after German bank robbery, suspects on run

items.title: Independent review finds five adverse findings against former Australian National University council members
items.summary: An independent review into 36 allegations concerning the Australian National University has made a series of adverse findings against former council members.

items.title: Man 'assisting detectives' with investigation into death of woman found on Henley Beach Esplanade
items.summary: Police say a man is speaking to them in relation to the death of a woman whose body was found outside a block of units overlooking Henley Beach on Thursday.

items.summary: The chief engineer of a Chinese-owned oil tanker attacked near the Strait of Hormuz says it is "still unclear" who fired on the vessel.

items.title: Closing statements heard in Charlotte MacInnes's defamation case against Rebel Wilson
items.summary: Final arguments in the defamation trial between Charlotte MacInnes and Rebel Wilson have heard claims of a suspicious career jump to "terrible, terrible" allegations.

items.title: Inquest told Queensland prisoner Glen Francis died weeks after pain dismissed as ‘drug-seeking behaviour’

items.title: Why there's no end in sight for Kezie Apps, Origin's ageless wonder

items.summary: The US and Iran both blame each other for initiating renewed attacks as tensions in the Strait of Hormuz ratchet up, threatening the ceasefire.

items.summary: The former foreign minister had previously rejected calls from university staff and students to resign.
